label: The static finite-β m=1 obstruction is genuine and well-characterized — it survives as a special-case limit
description: The classical Newcomb-Kadomtsev result establishes that the static finite-pressure Z-pinch is linearly unstable to m=1. This is reproduced and benchmarked by Atlas (the ARPACK pipeline recovers Newcomb 1960 eigenvalues to 10^-5 relative accuracy). The result is not weakened by Atlas — it is contextualized as the limit-case where H3 is absent (monotonic F profile). Bondeson-Iacono's toroidal flow analysis sits in an adjacent regime where rotation mitigates but doesn't eliminate the kink, again because the H3 ingredient is missing. The static obstruction is genuine and remains the correct prediction in its native regime.

scope.regime: static or non-helical-rotating Z-pinch at finite pressure
scope.framework: Frieman-Rotenberg self-adjoint MHD operator (no flow) → Newcomb counting theorem
conditions: no rotation OR rotation without H3

contributing_claims.label: Static Z-pinch at finite pressure cannot be linearly stable to m=1 in ideal MHD
contributing_claims.rewrite_description: N_01 narrowed: m=1 obstruction holds in STATIC Z-pinch at finite pressure (out-of-regime weak for rotating helical).
contributing_claims.weakness_invoked: static assumption — extension to rotating helical is non-trivial

contributing_claims.label: Number of unstable eigenvalues equals number of interior nodes of marginal mode (Newcomb counting)
Linear MHD stability with stationary flow is governed by a non-Hermitian operator (overstability possible)
In toroidal/cylindrical geometry, rotation mitigates but does not eliminate kink at finite pressure
contributing_claims.rewrite_description: B_01 narrowed: toroidal rotation alone (without H3 quartic + helical shear) mitigates but doesn't eliminate kink.
contributing_claims.weakness_invoked: no non-monotonic Bz or helical sheared flow tested

contributing_claims.label: Linear shear flow alone does NOT stabilize m=1 kink across realistic Z-pinch profile classes
contributing_claims.rewrite_description: AN_01 narrowed: linear axial shear alone (without H3 quartic structure) does not stabilize m=1 across tested profile families.
contributing_claims.weakness_invoked: tested profile families lack H3 quartic minimum — Atlas's structural ingredient

contributing_claims.label: Kadomtsev's interchange criterion has an entropy-gradient interpretation analogous to Schwarzschild-Ledoux

contributing_evidence.label: Static finite-β Z-pinch has unstable m=1 spectrum (Suydam-style)
Toroidal rotation mitigates but doesn't eliminate kink (cylindrical analog)
m=1 kink remains unstable γ ~ O(v_i/a) at all tested shear levels on realistic profiles
ARPACK recovers Newcomb 1960 m=1 eigenvalues to 1e-5 relative accuracy
Violating H3 (monotonic F) gives only n^-2 Suydam accumulation; γ ≈ 4.2e-3 τ_A^-1
Kadomtsev marginal-stable profile has entropy-gradient interpretation analogous to Schwarzschild-Ledoux

tensions_resolved.before: Three claims (N_01, AN_01, B_01) seemed to assert universal m=1 instability — Atlas would disagree
tensions_resolved.after: Each is now scope-narrowed to its actual tested regime; Atlas's regime is genuinely new and complementary
tensions_resolved.resolution: Scope narrowing without weakening the underlying observations

open_questions.question: Is there a unified statement that subsumes Newcomb-static, Atlas-H3, and Bondeson-toroidal into a single regime-parameterized theorem?
open_questions.feeds_into: Theoretical unification — currently each is its own theorem

suggested_next_steps.description: Identify the regime parameter (presence/absence of quartic F, M_A range, geometry) and write a master theorem with conditional clauses
Revisit Crews 2024's entropy-gradient interpretation — does it have an analog for kink modes that aligns with the H3 picture?

transitions_out.relation: limit case — special case where H3 is absent
complementary — m=0 stabilization closes a different mode, Newcomb-Kadomtsev silent on m=0 in this regime
